seoTitle: Shoal Point vs Shorncliffe — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Shoal Point and Shorncliffe. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Shoal Point vs Shorncliffe: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shoal Point and Shorncliffe.
heading: Shoal Point vs Shorncliffe — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shoal Point, Queensland and Shorncliffe, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shoal Point has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Shorncliffe (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shoal Point or Shorncliffe safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shoal Point has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Shorncliffe scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shoal Point scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Shorncliffe scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shoal Point or Shorncliffe?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shoal Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
